---
title: "DM_R050: Derivation Documentation Structure Rule"
subtitle: "Single-file vs directory structure for derivation documentation"
chapter: "CH02"
category: "rule"
number: "DM_R050"
date-created: "2025-12-24"
date-modified: "2025-12-24"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Data Management"
article-number: "Rule 50"
parent_principle: "MP064"
related_to:
  - "DM_R044": "Derivation Implementation Standard"
  - "DM_R049": "Derivation Consumer Documentation"
  - "DM_R041": "ETL Directory Structure"
  - "MP097": "Principle-Implementation Separation"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
---

# DM_R050: Derivation Documentation Structure Rule {#overview}

This rule defines when derivation documentation should use single-file vs directory structure, ensuring consistency across the CH13_derivations documentation.

## Rule Statement

Derivation documentation MUST use one of two structure types based on its nature:

1. **Reference Type** (`derivation-reference`): Single file for static reference documents
2. **Workflow Type** (`derivation-index`): Directory structure for multi-step processing workflows

## Two Documentation Types {#types}

| Type | `type` value | `structure` value | When to Use |
|------|--------------|-------------------|-------------|
| **Reference** | `derivation-reference` | `single-file` | Data dictionaries, schema definitions, static reference docs |
| **Workflow** | `derivation-index` | `directory` | Multi-step processing workflows with execution flow |

### Decision Criteria

Use **single-file** (`derivation-reference`) when:
- The document defines table structures or schemas
- Content is primarily lookup/reference information
- No sequential execution steps exist
- Cross-referencing within one document is beneficial

Use **directory** (`derivation-index`) when:
- The derivation has multiple processing steps (D0X_00, D0X_01, ...)
- An execution flow exists that should be documented separately
- Consumer modules need to be documented (DM_R049)
- Individual tasks exceed 200 lines each

## Directory Structure Pattern {#directory-pattern}

For workflow-type derivations (`derivation-index`), use this structure:

```
D{nn}_{name}/
├── index.qmd                         # Overview + metadata (REQUIRED)
├── D{nn}_00_{first_task}.qmd        # First task
├── D{nn}_01_{second_task}.qmd       # Second task
├── ...
├── D{nn}_XX_execution.qmd           # Execution flow (REQUIRED per DM_R044)
└── D{nn}_XX_module_consumers.qmd    # Consumer docs (REQUIRED per DM_R049)
```

### Required Files

| File | Purpose | Requirement |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `index.qmd` | Overview, metadata, flow diagram | REQUIRED |
| `D{nn}_XX_execution.qmd` | Master execution flow | REQUIRED (DM_R044) |
| `D{nn}_XX_module_consumers.qmd` | UI module consumers | REQUIRED (DM_R049) |
| `D{nn}_00_*.qmd` through `D{nn}_XX_*.qmd` | Task files | As needed |

## Required Metadata {#metadata}

### For Workflow Type (index.qmd)

```yaml
---
title: "D{nn}: {Name}"
subtitle: "{Description}"
chapter: "CH10"
category: "derivation"
number: "D{nn}"
type: "derivation-index"
structure: "directory"
derives_from:
  - "MP064": "ETL-Derivation Separation Principle"
  - "DM_R044": "Derivation Implementation Standard"
  - "DM_R049": "Derivation Consumer Documentation"
consumes:
  - "{upstream}": "{description}"
task_files:
  - "D{nn}_00_{task}.qmd"
  - "D{nn}_01_{task}.qmd"
  - "..."
---
```

### For Reference Type (single file)

```yaml
---
title: "D{nn}: {Name}"
subtitle: "{Description}"
chapter: "CH10"
category: "derivation"
number: "D{nn}"
type: "derivation-reference"
structure: "single-file"
note: "{Reason for single-file structure}"
tables_defined:
  - "{table_name}"
consumed_by:
  - "D{nn}": "{downstream derivation}"
---
```

## Examples {#examples}

### Reference Type: D00 (Data Dictionary)

D00_app_data_init.qmd is a reference document defining table structures:

```yaml
type: "derivation-reference"
structure: "single-file"
note: "Data dictionary - kept as single file for cross-reference convenience"
tables_defined:
  - "df_profile_by_customer"
  - "df_dna_by_customer"
  - "df_dna_by_customer"
consumed_by:
  - "D01": "DNA Analysis"
  - "D02": "Filtered Customer Views"
```

### Workflow Type: D01-D04 (Processing Workflows)

D01_dna_analysis/ through D04_poisson_marketing/ are workflow derivations:

```
D01_dna_analysis/
├── index.qmd                        # type: derivation-index
├── D01_00_consume_etl_output.qmd
├── D01_01_customer_aggregation.qmd
├── D01_02_rfm_calculation.qmd
├── D01_03_dna_analysis.qmd
├── D01_04_customer_profile.qmd
├── D01_05_app_views.qmd
├── D01_06_execution.qmd             # DM_R044 compliance
└── D01_07_module_consumers.qmd      # DM_R049 compliance
```

## Relationship to Other Principles {#relationships}

| Principle | Relationship |
|-----------|--------------|
| **DM_R044** | Defines the five-part structure for R implementation scripts; DM_R050 defines the `.qmd` documentation structure |
| **DM_R049** | Requires `D{nn}_XX_module_consumers.qmd` for workflow derivations |
| **DM_R041** | Defines directory structure for `update_scripts/`; DM_R050 extends to `docs/` |
| **MP097** | All `.qmd` files contain NSQL only, no R code |

## Compliance Checklist {#checklist}

### For Reference Type

- [ ] `type: "derivation-reference"` in YAML
- [ ] `structure: "single-file"` in YAML
- [ ] `note:` explaining why single-file
- [ ] `tables_defined:` if applicable
- [ ] `consumed_by:` listing downstream derivations

### For Workflow Type

- [ ] Directory created with `D{nn}_{name}/` pattern
- [ ] `index.qmd` with `type: "derivation-index"`
- [ ] `structure: "directory"` in YAML
- [ ] `task_files:` listing all task files
- [ ] `derives_from:` listing principles
- [ ] `consumes:` listing upstream dependencies
- [ ] `D{nn}_XX_execution.qmd` exists (DM_R044)
- [ ] `D{nn}_XX_module_consumers.qmd` exists (DM_R049)

## Rationale {#rationale}

1. **Reference documents** (like D00) benefit from single-file structure because:
   - Cross-referencing table definitions is easier in one file
   - Schema definitions are static and don't have execution flow
   - Searching for column names across tables is simplified

2. **Workflow documents** (like D01-D04) benefit from directory structure because:
   - Each task can be maintained independently
   - Execution flow documentation has a dedicated file
   - Consumer documentation follows DM_R049 pattern
   - Large workflows become manageable when split into task files
